arabic_text:
ولداً عن والد
هر درجہ میں دس دس درجہ ہیں
english_text:
DARJAAT — Stages in evolution & Progress from Momin towards BAB
Momin
Hudud (Mukasir)
Mazun (Mansoob)
Dai Mutlaq
Dai Balagh
Hujjat
Baab
IMAM
Baab Zahir
Baab Batin
Baab Salsal
Baab Imbeási
Baab ul Abwaab
(Son from father — each rank contains ten sub-ranks)
alt_text: An ascending staircase diagram titled 'DARJAAT' (Ranks). The eight main steps rise from 'Momin' (believer) at the bottom through 'Hudud (Mukasir)', 'Mazun (Mansoob)', 'Dai Mutlaq', 'Dai Balagh', 'Hujjat', 'Baab', up to 'IMAM' at the top. A side staircase branches from 'Baab' showing five sub-ranks (Baab Zahir → Baab Batin → Baab Salsal → Baab Imbeási → Baab ul Abwaab). An Urdu annotation notes that each rank itself contains ten sub-ranks; an Arabic note reads 'ولداً عن والد' (son from father — i.e., the lineage of transmission).
notes: Classic Ismaili da'wa hierarchy diagram. Lays out the eight ranks of spiritual progress from the lay believer (Momin) up to the Imam, with the Baab rank further subdivided into five 'doors'. The 'ولداً عن والد' line emphasizes the hereditary/transmitted chain of teaching authority.
